pattern: Find the minimum period length by checking each divisor of n: the string is a concatenation of anagrams iff every block of that length has the same character frequency.
hints: If s is formed by concatenating anagrams of t, then |t| must divide |s|. Try each divisor of |s| in increasing order.
For a candidate length d, split s into |s|/d chunks and check if every chunk has the same character frequency distribution as the first chunk.
Return the first (smallest) divisor that passes the check; no need to check non-divisors.

edge_inputs.description: all distinct chars, answer equals full length
length is prime, only divisors are 1 and n

stress.description: construct s as repeated anagrams of a base string
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    t_len = rng.randint(1, min(size // 2, 10))
    t = list(''.join(rng.choice('abcdef') for _ in range(t_len)))
    reps = rng.randint(1, size // t_len)
    chunks = []
    for _ in range(reps):
        perm = t[:]
        rng.shuffle(perm)
        chunks.append(''.join(perm))
    return [''.join(chunks)]
